acceptance_targets: [chapter_04_prose_001] PASS if the revised passages at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:37, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:77, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:167, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:235, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:351 each read as complete narrative sentences with explicit subjects and verbs where needed; FAIL if any of those moments still depend on one-word narration or omitted-verbless shorthand.

[chapter_04_prose_002] PASS if no more than one of the cited passages at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:135, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:173, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:195, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:285 still begins with a demonstrative reaction sentence and each revised beat is distinguishable on the page through a different physical, spatial, or behavioral response; FAIL if multiple cited beats still rely on the same "That ..." interpretive construction.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if the revised passages at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:37, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:77, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:167, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:235, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:351 each read as complete narrative sentences with explicit subjects and verbs where needed; FAIL if any of those moments still depend on one-word narration or omitted-verbless shorthand.
findings.evidence: Repeated clipped narration appears at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:37, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:77, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:167, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:235, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:351.
findings.problem: The chapter repeatedly drops into clipped narrative shorthand such as "A swallow," verbless countdown fragments, and one-word reaction beats. That cadence conflicts with the constitution's ban on elliptical clipping and starts to read as mannerism rather than earned compression.
findings.rewrite_direction: At snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:37, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:77, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:167, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:235, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:351, recast the fragment beats as fully grammatical narrative sentences while preserving speed and pressure. Keep the same phone-call, countdown, threat, guard, and timing functions, but give each beat a complete clause instead of relying on clipped shorthand.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if no more than one of the cited passages at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:135, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:173, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:195, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:285 still begins with a demonstrative reaction sentence and each revised beat is distinguishable on the page through a different physical, spatial, or behavioral response; FAIL if multiple cited beats still rely on the same "That ..." interpretive construction.
findings.evidence: A repeated demonstrative-response formula appears at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:135, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:173, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:195, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:285.
findings.problem: Several leverage shifts are translated through the same interpretive sentence shape, opening with "That ..." to explain how a line lands. Used this often, the formula becomes a prose tic and slightly over-explains moments that the chapter could otherwise land through sensation, action, or silence.
findings.rewrite_direction: At snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:135, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:173, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:195, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:285, vary or cut the demonstrative reaction openings. Replace at least three of those beats with distinct renderings rooted in Marisol's body, the room, or the other character's behavior so each turn of pressure lands in a different sentence shape.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

revision_pass.focus: Focus on prose and copyedit finish: diction precision, rhythm control, sentence-level clarity, grammar, line-level polish without flattening style, and explicit aesthetic-risk audit (sanitization is the primary risk — do not soften or euphemize; push toward creative risk and specificity).
revision_pass.label: prose/copyedit fixes

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: Lean into the novel's uncomfortable overlap of sanitation, state violence, labor dependence, and family love. When an edge case appears, prefer the version that keeps physical consequence, class coercion, and emotional contradiction visible on the page rather than the version that makes characters cleaner, nobler, or easier to forgive.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: If a scene turns away at the exact moment bodily, economic, or moral consequence should become legible, revise toward specificity. Omission is only valid when residue, aftermath, and changed behavior keep the pressure fully present.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: High - contractions are the spoken default; uncontracted forms should mark emphasis, bureaucracy, class performance, or character-specific formality.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: Alternate dense procedural chapters with quieter aftermath, meals, night holds, confession scenes, and dark-comic institutional theater. No two consecutive chapters should rely on the same rhythm of scene count, dialogue density, or pressure delivery; vary between confrontation, drift, revelation, ritual preparation, humiliation, public spectacle, and stillness under pressure.
prose_style_profile.diction: Concrete, infrastructural, unsentimental, and specific about procedures without becoming jargon-drunk. Abstract thought should rise from physical consequence instead of replacing it.
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: Close third anchored primarily to Marisol's bodily labor, practical thought, and moral self-management, with any widening kept chapter-level and earned by scene design.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Let long freighted sentences carry labor process, institutional language, or accumulating sensory load; then cut to shorter, grammatically complete sentences at impact points where a body, fact, or decision can no longer be blurred.
